This behavior is federally regulated and banned on Kalshi.

We’ve taken on a number of political insider trading cases. Staffers should be careful before trying to turn a buck based on insider info, or they could end up in serious trouble. It won't be worth it.

This is what years of hard work and doing things the right way look like.

We’re by far the largest prediction market in America, and we achieved that while making sure to get federally regulated, operating legally onshore, and standing by our values when listing markets.

Kalshi is increasingly being used because its data is extremely effective at predicting what’s next – finding homes at trusted newsrooms, in campaign offices, with everyday people, and with everyone in between from Wall Street to Main Street. You can see what people think in real-time on issues that matter.

Now, we’re moving from early adoption to accelerating institutional adoption. We’re just getting started.
We don’t wait for problems. We build systems to prevent them. https://t.co/psYsTp0jzD

Prediction markets are simple: You trade on if you think something will happen.

Will inflation go up? Will it rain tomorrow? Who will top Spotify’s charts?

Prices reflect what the masses believe.

Don’t mess with our enforcement team.

Here’s how we caught an insider trader in a Texas primary: https://t.co/y7czzANPhb

A candidate for California Governor was caught trading on his own election last spring.

This is how Kalshi caught him. 🧵 https://t.co/D3ixZfBWvF

This is what we love to see: our leaders taking insider trading as seriously as we do.

On Kalshi, elected officials and their staff are already banned from insider trading nationwide. This amendment strengthens that ban and makes prediction markets even freer and fairer.
The wisdom of crowds is timeless.

This is why it matters if a prediction market is federally regulated.

📹 Head of Enforcement @robertjdenault on Bloomberg https://t.co/yk9428Wyjf
